arguments.task: Open official Xero developer pages and read canonical pages that support: product/API description, authentication methods, API types/endpoints, rate limits, and any official pricing or access/gating information for developers. Prefer pages under developer.xero.com or official Xero domains. Return the final URLs visited and concise extracted facts with quotations or close paraphrases. Also check whether Xero offers an official MCP server or Model Context Protocol documentation on first-party pages.

input.thought: Start a browser run on official Xero sources to collect verifiable evidence from canonical pages.

raw_result.description: Cloud accounting and business-platform APIs for accounting, payroll, assets, projects, files, and related financial workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs describe OAuth 2.0 flows including authorization code, PKCE, and Custom Connections; no first-party evidence was gathered for API keys, Basic auth, or standalone personal access tokens for the public API.
raw_result.gating_reason: Xero publishes developer pricing tiers on its official pricing page (Starter, Core, Plus, Advanced, Enterprise) with connection limits and data-egress-based fees, indicating production-scale API access is on paid plans rather than a permanently free self-serve tier.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs across multiple product areas including Accounting plus additional business domains such as payroll, assets, projects, and files; docs also include OAuth-specific limits guidance.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Xero’s official developer site has an AI page that links to an open-source official MCP server repository (XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server).

raw_result.blocker: Buildable from public docs and OAuth 2.0, but practical deployment may require selecting a paid pricing tier as connection volume and data egress grow.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits page documents concurrent, per-minute, daily, and app-wide limits, with HTTP error/headers behavior when limits are exceeded; the browser evidence also indicated different limits/allowances tied to pricing tiers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-developed official developer portal, explicit OAuth 2.0 guidance, published rate-limit documentation, dedicated pricing page, and an official MCP server signal strong platform maturity for agent tooling.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered evidence indicates paid pricing tiers and OAuth2 clearly, but the browser run did not return verbatim final extracted text for all structured pricing/access details. Access-tier and some rate-limit nuance were inferred from pages visited rather than directly quoted in the final output, so a human should verify the exact gating interpretation from the live pricing page.
